execution_details.stdout:
Setting system policy to DEFAULT
Note: System-wide crypto policies are applied on application start-up.
It is recommended to restart the system for the change of policies
to fully take place.

llm_verdict.message: ✅ The system cryptographic policy was set to **DEFAULT**. No additional remediation steps are needed.

We need to decide. Probably set to DEFAULT. Since rule wants system cryptography policy to use ciphers from policy. Using default policy is fine. So set to DEFAULT.

Thus Plan: run update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT. Also possibly check if command available: dnf provides. But we can assume it's installed.

So plan: (1) Use read_file maybe to check /usr/bin/update-crypto-policies existence? But we can just run. But instructions: always use read_file before modifying config file. No config file we modify. So we skip reading.

Thus plan: Use run_cmd to set policy.

OK produce plan.

Let's call review_plan.

The plan is approved. We should run the command: update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT. According to the remediation script, var_system_crypto_policy='' indicating no policy value, but we want to set to DEFAULT. Usually, default is DEFAULT. Might also be set to DEFAULT as a default baseline. We'll run `update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT`. Ensure we capture output? We only need to run the command. We need to respond with final output. The instructions: we must plan, etc. Already complete. But final answer likely includes no further conversation because we executed. According to guidelines, just output final message.
